paper.title: Inference-Time Scaling for Complex Tasks: Where We Stand and What Lies Ahead

claims.text: Inference-time scaling improves performance across tasks but effectiveness varies by domain with diminishing returns as complexity increases
Higher token consumption does not indicate higher accuracy across models; substantial token usage variability exists even for correct answers
Continued scaling with perfect verifiers or superscaling (50x calls) consistently improves performance and allows conventional models to approach reasoning model results on some tasks

methods.name: Toy reproduction experiment
methods.description: Simplified numpy simulation of the paper's key claim about inference-time-scaling-llms: Inference-time scaling improves performance across tasks but effectiveness varies by domain with diminishing returns as
methods.runnable_hint: Build a tiny synthetic task where multiple random candidate solutions are sampled (best-of-n / inference-time scaling). Score each with a simple verifier, pick the best, and report accuracy vs n_candidates. Metric: accuracy_at_n vs n_candidates.

params.name: n_trials
params.description: number of synthetic problems to evaluate

params.name: noise
params.description: difficulty / noise level of the synthetic task
